summarization.a: The line chart illustrates the change in QCM mass gain over time, showing a stepwise increase at regular intervals.

a.question: At what temperature was this experiment conducted?
How many ALD cycles were performed?
What was the plasma exposure time during one ALD cycle for this experiment?
What information does this figure provide about the ALD growth behaviour?
a.answer: The figure illustrates how the mass of the growing film evolves during ten ALD cycles at a substrate temperature of 70 °C when a non-saturated aluminium precursor dose is used in each cycle. The quartz crystal microbalance data show a clear stepwise increase in mass, confirming that material is added incrementally during every ALD cycle. A transient negative mass change appears during each oxygen plasma step; however, this is attributed to a plasma-induced measurement artifact rather than actual material loss.
